title: Cookies, Authentication, Private Data, Write Avoidance, and Session Cleanup · Program Forensics
summary: A preserved crawl trace contains one plausible authorization, URL, robots, HTTP, decoding, parser, selector, frontier, concurrency, metadata, accessibility, privacy, export, browser, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized session and data-custody contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Use a dedicated cookie jar, inspect domain, path, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, partition, expiry, and redirect scope, and avoid ambient sharing.
Use dedicated least-privilege identities, approved secret sources, documented pages and fields, rotation, revocation, and audit ownership.
Restrict crawler navigation to safe retrieval and block logout, unsubscribe, cart, form, API mutation, and side-effecting endpoints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Session Cleanup Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence-1ecbk0" that uses case 7581 to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, contact sites, import or claim native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, or Playwright behavior, read or write host files, use credentials, launch browsers, schedule host jobs, or claim live accessibility, privacy, legal, load, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, the recorded stable package versions, fixed and malformed fixtures, fake transports, controlled local servers, authorized disposable target sites, representative browser profiles, privacy, security, accessibility and legal review, load, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Session Cleanup Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Session Cleanup Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Authorized crawling and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Session Cleanup Evidence, keep Session Cleanup Evidence connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence-1ecbk0
# Competency: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence.
# Case 7581: a clean package and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def evidence_scraper_session_cleanup_evidence_1ecbk0(authorized_pages: int = 24, collected_pages: int = 24, retained_private_fields: int = 4, approved_private_fields: int = 4, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ecbk0 = "1ecbk0-0kbce1-7581"
    assert evidence_variant_1ecbk0
    evidence_axes_1ecbk0 = (
        "authorization1ecbk0",
        "url7581",
        "response0kbce1",
        "tree1ecbk07581",
        "frontier75811ecbk0",
        "report0kbce17581",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ecbk0)) == 6
    if authorized_pages < 1 or collected_pages < 0:
        return False, collected_pages, "session-count"
    if collected_pages != authorized_pages:
        return False, collected_pages, "authorization-scope"
    if retained_private_fields != approved_private_fields:
        return False, retained_private_fields, "private-field-scope"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert collected_pages == authorized_pages and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, collected_pages, "authorized-session-custody"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

debug case — A debug site-quality team handles case 4193 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “Process exit reliably clears every credential and browser storage artifact.”?

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Session Cleanup Evidence · fault isolation · Debug Session Cleanup E….
